response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile generalist engineer with genuine IDE-internals exposure (C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI, a C IDE) and excellent software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing). However, the role requires deep JVM expertise, strong Java and Kotlin, and solid Spring/enterprise-framework experience, and the resume shows almost none of this: the dominant stack is JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with Java appearing only incidentally and no Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, or Quarkus at all. The core required qualifications are largely unmet.
strengths: Direct IDE/dev-tooling experience writing C/C++ for the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Broad systems experience including low-latency, high-throughput services
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience
Java appears only incidentally; no evidence of deep JVM expertise
Primary stack (TS/Node/DLang) misaligned with JVM tooling focus
